sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 87:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 87:1
items.html: Because the vessel lacks its primary record, which is a record of extension, and the record of clothing that remains in it is considered the posterior of the vessel of Keter. Also, her light up in the Rosh stands with its posterior to the branches in the Guf. (There)
